Description: As a user, I want to upload 1–4 CVs so that the system can extract and consolidate my career data into a Master Profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can drag-drop or select files (PDF, DOCX, JPEG/PNG/TIFF, plain text)
- POST /api/profile/upload accepts multipart/form-data with optional job_id query param
- System detects file format and applies appropriate extraction strategy:
  - PDF with text layer → pymupdf
  - DOCX → python-docx
  - Scanned PDF / images → OCR via CVImageExtractor (MistralVision default, Tesseract opt-in)
  - Plain text → direct parse
- LLM extraction uses JD_AWARE_CV_EXTRACTION_PROMPT (if job_id provided) or GENERIC_CV_EXTRACTION_PROMPT
- CVUploadResponse returned: profile_id, status (DRAFT if completeness < 0.5 or conflicts, else COMPLETE), completeness_score, conflicts[], enrichment_record_id, expires_at
- Upload record created in uploads table: original_filename, content_hash (SHA-256), mime_type, file_path, byte_size, llm_tokens_used, llm_provider
- File stored via StorageProvider abstraction (LocalStorageProvider default, S3 for Cloud)
- 7-day GDPR TTL set on expires_at (enforced by Retention Worker)
- Multiple CVs trigger merge via MergeStrategy (ADR-013)
- Progress indicator shown during parsing
- Success message displays data points extracted and completeness score

Description: As a user with multiple CV versions, I want the system to automatically accumulate and enrich my Master Profile so it captures my complete career history without losing valuable context.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System accumulates data from multiple CVs into a single Master Profile (ADR-013)
- Role titles for the same position are accumulated into role_aliases[]
- Responsibility and achievement bullets are unioned and semantically deduplicated
- Skill proficiency is updated to the highest value found
- Company name variants are normalized
- Only truly factual irreconcilable differences (e.g., contradicting start/end dates) are flagged as conflicts
- Master Profile created and displayed to user

Description: As the system, I need to detect and flag conflicts in merged CV data so users can resolve them in context later
Acceptance Criteria:
- Conflicts stored in master_profile.conflicts (JSONB)
- Each conflict includes: field, conflicting values, source CVs, priority level
- Conflicts marked as 'flagged' or 'deferred'
- Conflict resolution strategy stored for later use

Description: As an international candidate, I want the system to detect my source market (India, US, Brazil) and flag cultural adaptation items so I know what needs to change for DACH
Acceptance Criteria:
- System detects source market based on CV structure, content patterns, metadata
- Cultural flags identified: PII to remove, photo recommendation, format restructuring, credential mapping, regulatory framework mapping
- Auto-resolves non-controversial items (e.g., removes declaration, strips father's name)
- Flags items requiring user input
- Cultural Readiness Score calculated and displayed

User Story Title: Parse job description from URL or text
Description: As a user, I want to input a job description (URL or paste text) so the system can analyze requirements
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can paste JD URL or text
- System scrapes URL if provided (graceful fallback to manual paste if scraping fails)
- JD text extracted and stored
- Analysis initiated automatically

User Story Title: Analyze JD for required skills and experience
Description: As the system, I need to extract key requirements from the JD so I can identify gaps in the user's profile
Acceptance Criteria:
- System identifies required skills, certifications, experience levels
- Regulatory frameworks detected (e.g., GAMP 5, 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP)
- Team size/leadership requirements extracted
- Years of experience requirements identified
- Match score calculated against Master Profile (0-100%)

Description: As an international candidate, I want the system to identify how my international regulatory experience maps to EU/DACH frameworks so I can position it correctly
Acceptance Criteria:
- System detects source regulatory frameworks (ANVISA, FDA, WHO-GMP, CDSCO, etc.)
- Maps to DACH/EU equivalents (EU GMP, EMA, etc.)
- Confidence level assigned to each mapping
- Flagged for user confirmation if confidence <75%
- Mapping stored for CV generation

User Story Title: Generate interview questions for gaps (Full Interview Mode)
Description: As the system, I need to generate a comprehensive sequence of interview questions that help users fill knowledge gaps and resolve conflicts for the target role, prioritized by information value.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Interview operates in two modes:
  - MODE A (Targeted): For users with existing profiles (completeness ≥ 0.3). Soft target 3–8 questions, hard ceiling 12.
  - MODE B (Guided): For new users (completeness < 0.3). Soft target 10–15 questions, hard ceiling 20.
- Gap categories classified as:
  - Category A: Confirmed matches (no question generated)
  - Category B: Inferred matches requiring confirmation (e.g., 'Your CV mentions MES — did you work with batch record validation?')
  - Category C: Gaps or unstated experience (open-ended exploratory questions)
- Question order prioritizes information gain:
  - Category C (exploratory) questions first — capture unstated experience with highest information value
  - Category B (confirmation) questions second — validate inferred experience
- Each question includes context (why this matters for the role)
- Questions are open-ended (free text, 2–3 sentences expected)
- Estimated time shown upfront (1.2 min per question)
- Interview state persisted; user can resume from interruption

User Story Title: Generate interview questions for gaps (Full Interview Mode)
Description: As the system, I need to generate a comprehensive sequence of interview questions that help users fill knowledge gaps and resolve conflicts for the target role, prioritized by information value.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Interview operates in two modes:
  - MODE A (Targeted): For users with existing profiles (completeness ≥ 0.3). Soft target 3–8 questions, hard ceiling 12.
  - MODE B (Guided): For new users (completeness < 0.3). Soft target 10–15 questions, hard ceiling 20.
- Gap categories classified as:
  - Category A: Confirmed matches (no question generated)
  - Category B: Inferred matches requiring confirmation (e.g., 'Your CV mentions MES — did you work with batch record validation?')
  - Category C: Gaps or unstated experience (open-ended exploratory questions)
- Question order prioritizes information gain:
  - Category C (exploratory) questions first — capture unstated experience with highest information value
  - Category B (confirmation) questions second — validate inferred experience
- Each question includes context (why this matters for the role)
- Questions are open-ended (free text, 2–3 sentences expected)
- Estimated time shown upfront (1.2 min per question)
- Interview state persisted; user can resume from interruption

Description: As the system, I need to surface job-relevant CV conflicts as strategic questions during the interview so users can resolve them in context while tailoring for the target role.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Interview orchestrator identifies job-relevant conflicts from `master_profile.conflicts`
- For each relevant conflict, generates a strategic question framed as an optimization opportunity (e.g., 'Which title better reflects your management responsibilities for this role?')
- Questions include: conflicting values, source CVs, and strategic context tied to the target JD
- User provides explicit answer (selects preferred value or provides clarification)
- ResponseParser extracts user intent and returns a `ConflictResolution` object
- ProfileUpdater calls `POST /api/profile/conflicts/{conflict_id}/resolve` endpoint with extracted intent
- Resolution logged with `source: interview_session_id` in the `EnrichmentRecord` for full audit trail
- Non-job-relevant conflicts remain unresolved in `pending_conflicts` (not surfaced, deferred to future applications)
- User can view all pending conflicts anytime via `GET /api/profile`

Description: As the system, I need to ask users to resolve CV conflicts in a job-contextualized way so they make strategic choices
Acceptance Criteria:
- Conflicts filtered for job relevance (only relevant conflicts asked)
- Questions frame conflict as optimization opportunity, not data error
- Each question includes: conflicting values, source CVs, strategic context
- User can select preferred value or provide clarification
- Resolution stored in master_profile

User Story Title: Generate interview questions for cultural adaptation
Description: As an international candidate, I want the system to ask me questions about cultural adaptation (photo, credentials, language, work auth) so I can make informed choices for DACH
Acceptance Criteria:
- Questions generated based on cultural flags
- Topics include: photo preference, credential mapping, language proficiency, work authorization, availability
- Each question includes DACH context and recommendations
- User responses stored in master_profile.cultural_context
- Responses inform CV generation (e.g., photo placement, credential presentation)

Description: As a user, I want to answer interview questions in a conversational chat interface so the experience feels natural and engaging
Acceptance Criteria:
- Chat UI displays one question at a time
- Progress bar shows 'Question X of Y'
- User can submit free-text answers
- Answers saved automatically
- Option to skip or return to previous questions
- Session state persisted (user can close browser and resume)

Description: As the system, I need to auto-detect whether the user should receive a Targeted interview (MODE A) or a Guided interview (MODE B) based on profile completeness.
Acceptance Criteria:
- calculate_completeness() evaluated at interview session creation
- MODE A (Targeted): completeness ≥ MODE_B_COMPLETENESS_THRESHOLD (0.3). Consumes GapAnalysis. Soft target 3–8 questions, hard ceiling 12. C→B question ordering.
- MODE B (Guided): completeness < 0.3. No GapAnalysis required. Section-by-section build plan. Soft target 10–15 questions, hard ceiling 20.
- Mode stored on interview_session record
- Mode displayed to user: 'Quick Interview (5 min)' vs 'Profile Builder (15 min)'
- Mode cannot change mid-session

User Story Title: MODE B guided interview builds profile section-by-section
Description: As a new user with no CV, I want a guided interview that builds my Master Profile section by section so I can create a professional profile from scratch.
Acceptance Criteria:
- MODE B interview generates questions organized by profile section: Personal Summary, Work Experience, Skills & Technologies, Certifications, Education, Languages
- Questions are open-ended and exploratory (e.g., 'Tell me about your most recent position and what you were responsible for')
- Each answer parsed and mapped to Master Profile fields
- Section completeness shown as user progresses
- Soft target 10–15 questions, hard ceiling 20
- Profile completeness score updated after each answer
- At completion, Master Profile is populated sufficiently for CV generation

Description: As a user, I want to resume an interrupted interview from where I left off so I don't lose progress
Acceptance Criteria:
- CV generated using Master Profile + interview answers + JD analysis
- Content prioritized based on job relevance
- Regulatory frameworks mapped and positioned
- Format applied (DACH reverse-chronological for Priya, standard for Marcus/Emma)
- PDF generated using Playwright headless Chromium from Jinja2 templates
- HTML preview served to frontend iframe uses the same Jinja2 HTML/CSS as PDF generation
- Generation is asynchronous; user/agent will poll for status

User Story Title: Generate tailored CV from Master Profile + JD
Description: As the system, I need to create a job-tailored CV using the enriched Master Profile and interview answers
Acceptance Criteria:
- CV generated using Master Profile + interview answers + JD analysis
- Content prioritized based on job relevance
- Regulatory frameworks mapped and positioned
- Format applied (DACH reverse-chronological for Priya, standard for Marcus/Emma)
- Generation is asynchronous: generate_cv initiates background job, returns {cv_id, status: 'pending', expires_at}
- Frontend polls get_cv_status(cv_id) until status 'ready' or 'failed' (polling interval ~2s)
- MCP agents use same polling pattern via get_cv_status tool
- PDF generated using Jinja2 templates + Playwright headless Chromium
- HTML preview served to frontend iframe uses same Jinja2 HTML/CSS as PDF
- Generated PDF stored with TTL: 90 days (human), 24 hours (agent)
- Auto-deleted by Retention Worker when expires_at past

User Story Title: Preview CV in browser before download
Description: As a user, I want to see a preview of my tailored CV in the browser so I can review it before downloading
Acceptance Criteria:
- CV preview loaded in iframe
- User can scroll through full CV
- Preview reflects all tailoring (gaps filled, conflicts resolved, cultural adaptations applied)
- 'Download PDF' button available
- 'Edit' option available for minor tweaks

Description: As a user, I want to download my tailored CV as a PDF so I can send it to the recruiter
Acceptance Criteria:
- PDF downloads with filename: Kandidatenprofil_[Name]_[JobTitle]_[Company].pdf
- PDF quality is professional (fonts, spacing, formatting preserved)
- File size reasonable (<5MB)
- Download completes without errors

Description: As a user, I want to make minor edits to my CV preview so I can fix typos or adjust wording before submitting
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can click on CV sections to edit
- Inline editor appears for selected text
- Changes reflected in preview immediately
- Edited CV regenerated with changes
- Download reflects all edits

Description: As a user, I want to see my profile completeness and recent applications on a dashboard so I know what's ready and what needs work
Acceptance Criteria:
- Dashboard loads with personalized greeting
- Profile completeness badge shown (e.g., '82% complete')
- Recent applications listed with status (Submitted, Draft, Incomplete)
- 'New Application' button prominent
- Quick stats: CVs generated, applications submitted

Description: As a user, I want to see all CVs I've generated and track which version I sent to which employer
Acceptance Criteria:
- Application history shows: Job title, Company, Date generated, Status
- Each application links to CV version
- Option to view, download, or regenerate CV
- Delta view available (compare CV v1 vs v2 for same JD)

Description: As the system, I need to record when a user downloads a CV so we can track pipeline status
Acceptance Criteria:
- Submission logged with: Job title, Company, Date, CV version, Status = 'Submitted'
- User can update status (Submitted, Interview Scheduled, Offer, Rejected, Withdrawn)
- Notes field available for user comments
- Dashboard updated in real-time

Description: As an international candidate, I want to see my Cultural Readiness Score on the dashboard so I know how well my profile is adapted for DACH
Acceptance Criteria:
- Score displayed as percentage (0-100%)
- Breakdown shown: PII removal, format, credentials, regulatory mapping, language, photo, work auth
- Suggestions for improvement listed
- Score updates after each interview/application

Description: As a recruiter, I want to generate an anonymized Kandidatenprofil so I can submit blind profiles to clients
Acceptance Criteria:
- Anonymization toggle available in CV generation screen
- When enabled: name, photo, contact details removed
- Experience data preserved (positions, projects, skills, certifications)
- Anonymized CV shows 'Candidate Profile: Anonymous'
- Filename reflects anonymization: Kandidatenprofil_Anonymous_[JobTitle]_[Company].pdf

Description: As a recruiter, I want to create a B2B account for my agency so I can manage candidates and mandates
Acceptance Criteria:
- B2B signup form: Company name, VAT ID (optional), email, password
- Account created with B2B workspace
- Agency profile setup initiated

Description: As a recruiter, I want to upload my agency's logo, template, and brand colors so all Kandidatenprofile are branded consistently
Acceptance Criteria:
- Logo upload (PNG, max 5MB)
- Kandidatenprofil template upload (DOCX with placeholder fields)
- Brand colors input (hex codes or auto-detect from logo)
- Preview of branded template
- Settings saved to agency profile

Description: As a recruiter, I want to create and manage a list of active client mandates so I can match candidates to them
Acceptance Criteria:
- Mandate creation: Job title, Company, JD text/URL, salary range, requirements
- Mandate list view with status (Open, Filled, On Hold)
- Search and filter by company, job title, date
- Mandate details editable
- Mandates available for candidate matching

Description: As a recruiter, I want to upload candidate CVs (1 or multiple) so the system can parse them and create Master Profiles
Acceptance Criteria:
- Single CV upload: drag-drop or file picker
- Batch upload: drag-drop multiple files (up to 10 CVs)
- Progress indicator for each file
- All CVs parsed in parallel
- Success message with count of profiles created

Description: As a recruiter, I want to see all candidate profiles in my workspace so I can search and filter for matching mandates
Acceptance Criteria:
- Candidate list with: Name, Position, Experience level, Key skills, Last updated
- Search by name, skill, company, position
- Filter by experience level, certifications, regulatory expertise
- Sort by date added, relevance
- Click to view full profile

Description: As a recruiter, I want to match multiple candidates against multiple mandates so I can identify the best fits quickly
Acceptance Criteria:
- Batch match: Select 5-10 candidates + 2-3 mandates
- System runs match analysis for each combination
- Results displayed as matrix: Candidates × Mandates with match scores
- Sort by score, filter by threshold (e.g., >75%)
- Select top matches for Kandidatenprofil generation

Description: As a recruiter, I want to generate branded Kandidatenprofile for matched candidates so I can submit them to clients
Acceptance Criteria:
- Select matched candidates from batch results
- System generates Kandidatenprofil for each using agency template
- Candidate details auto-filled
- Relevant experience highlighted based on mandate
- Anonymization option available
- Bulk download all profiles as ZIP

User Story Title: Manually select candidates and mandates for bulk Kandidatenprofil generation
Description: As a recruiter, I want to manually select candidates and mandates so I can generate multiple Kandidatenprofile at once without automated matching or scoring.
Acceptance Criteria:
- User selects multiple candidates from the candidate pool.
- User selects one or more mandates to match against.
- System confirms selected candidates and mandates.
- No automated matching algorithms or scoring is performed at this stage.
- Proceeds to bulk Kandidatenprofil generation based on manual selection.

User Story Title: Automated batch candidate-mandate matching with scoring
Description: As a recruiter, I want the system to automatically match multiple candidates against multiple mandates and provide match scores so I can quickly identify the best fits.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System performs match analysis for selected candidates and mandates.
- Results displayed as matrix showing Candidates × Mandates with match scores (e.g., 0-100%).
- Option to sort by score, filter by threshold (e.g., >75%).
- This feature is classified as High-Risk under EU AI Act Category 4 (Employment) and requires full compliance before deployment.
- Profiling of natural persons occurs, so Art. 6.3 exemption is not applicable.
Status: Deferred — EU AI Act compliance required

User Story Title: Capture GDPR consent for first-time candidates
Description: As a recruiter, I want to generate branded Kandidatenprofile for manually selected candidates so I can submit them to clients.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Select candidates who have been manually matched with a mandate (via US028a).
- System generates a branded Kandidatenprofil for each selected candidate using the agency template.
- Candidate details are auto-filled and relevant experience is highlighted based on the mandate.
- Anonymization option is available.
- Bulk download all profiles as a ZIP file.
- Note: This user story explicitly *excludes* any automated matching, scoring, or ranking logic. It relies solely on the recruiter's manual selection.

Description: As the system, I need to handle cases where candidates decline consent so we comply with GDPR
Acceptance Criteria:
- If consent declined: CV data deleted, profile not created
- User notified: 'Candidate declined consent'
- Option to request manual consent or try again later
- Audit log records decline

Description: As a recruiter, I want to delete a candidate's data from my workspace if they request it so we comply with GDPR
Acceptance Criteria:
- Candidate profile deletion option in settings
- Confirmation required: 'Are you sure? This cannot be undone.'
- All associated data deleted (CVs, Master Profile, interview sessions, submissions)
- Audit log records deletion with timestamp and reason
- Confirmation email sent to candidate

User Story Title: Generate API keys for agent access
Description: As a user, I want to generate API keys so I can authorize AI agents to access my Apliqa account
Acceptance Criteria:
- Settings page with 'API Keys' section
- 'Generate New Key' button
- Key displayed once (copy to clipboard)
- Key name and creation date shown
- Option to revoke keys
- Rate limits displayed (e.g., 100 calls/month)

User Story Title: Delegate CV generation to AI agent
Description: As a user, I want to authorize an AI agent to generate tailored CVs on my behalf so I can automate routine work
Acceptance Criteria:
- Agent authorization flow: User grants agent access to Master Profile + JD analysis
- Agent receives: Candidate profile data, JD, custom instructions
- Agent generates Kandidatenprofil and returns JSON
- User reviews and approves before submission
- Submission logged with 'Generated by Kaile' attribution

Description: As the system, I need row-level security (RLS) based multi-tenancy so each recruiter agency's data is fully isolated (ADR-011).
Acceptance Criteria:
- Tenant provisioned at B2B signup (linked to US023)
- tenant_id column on all tenant-scoped tables (candidates, mandates, submissions, generated_cvs)
- PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce data isolation: SET app.current_tenant_id per request
- API middleware sets tenant context from authenticated user's agency membership
- Tenant admin can invite/remove team members
- Member roles: admin, recruiter (read/write), viewer (read-only)
- Cross-tenant data access is impossible at the database level
- Tenant deletion cascades to all associated data with GDPR audit logging

Description: As a user, I want to see how many API calls my agents have made and how many credits I have remaining
Acceptance Criteria:
- Billing dashboard shows: API calls this month, credits remaining, cost per call
- Usage graph: calls over time
- Option to buy more credits
- Invoice history available

Description: As a new user, I want a guided onboarding experience so I understand how to use Apliqa effectively
Acceptance Criteria:
- Landing page with value prop and demo video
- Signup flow with email/password or SSO
- Onboarding checklist: Upload CV → Create Master Profile → Tailor for first role
- Tooltips and help text throughout
- Success celebrations at each milestone

User Story Title: Educational content on DACH CV norms
Description: As an international candidate, I want to learn about DACH CV conventions so I understand why the system is making certain recommendations
Acceptance Criteria:
- Help articles: 'German CV Format', 'Photo Guidelines', 'Credential Mapping', 'Work Authorization'
- Video tutorials: 'How to Adapt Your CV for DACH', 'Cultural Readiness Score Explained'
- FAQ section with common questions
- Links to external resources (anabin, DAAD, etc.)
- Contextual help in interview questions

Description: As a user, I want to see suggestions for improving my Master Profile so I can make my CVs stronger
Acceptance Criteria:
- Dashboard shows: 'Profile Completeness: 82%'
- Suggestions listed: 'Add certifications', 'Describe your FDA audit project', 'Update language skills'
- Click suggestion to add/edit data
- Completeness score updates after each change

Description: As a user, I want my tailored CV to generate quickly so I can download and submit it promptly
Acceptance Criteria:
- PDF generated using Playwright headless Chromium
- All fonts embedded
- Spacing and layout preserved from HTML
- No rendering errors or blank pages
- File size reasonable (<5MB)

Description: As a user, I want my PDF to render correctly with proper formatting, fonts, and spacing
Acceptance Criteria:
- PDF generated using WeasyPrint
- All fonts embedded
- Spacing and layout preserved from HTML
- No rendering errors or blank pages
- File size <5MB

Description: As a user, I want to use Apliqa on my phone so I can apply for jobs on the go
Acceptance Criteria:
- All screens responsive (mobile-first design)
- Touch-friendly buttons and inputs
- Interview chat works on mobile
- CV preview readable on small screens
- Download works on mobile browsers

User Story Title: Create flow session when user starts a new application
Description: As the system, I need to create a flow session when a user starts a new application so the entire journey is tracked as a state machine.
Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /api/flow creates a new flow_session row (one per user × job)
- Idempotent: returns existing active session if one exists for (user_id, job_id)
- Step graph initialized: jd_analysis → cv_import → gap_analysis → interview → cv_generation → complete
- user_type auto-detected at creation (new vs returning) based on existing Master Profile
- current_step set to first applicable step (returning users skip cv_import)
- available_actions computed for current step
- Session state persisted in flow_sessions table

Description: As the system, I need to enforce valid step transitions so users and agents cannot skip or repeat steps illegally.
Acceptance Criteria:
- VALID_TRANSITIONS dict enforced server-side (apliqa/services/flow/orchestrator.py)
- POST /api/flow/{session_id}/transition accepts target_step + artifact_id
- Each step must provide its artifact ID before transition is allowed
- Invalid transitions return HTTP 409 with explanation
- Backward transitions not allowed (except explicit restart)
- Transition logged with timestamp for audit trail

Description: As the system, I need to detect whether a user is new or returning so I can skip unnecessary steps and optimize the flow.
Acceptance Criteria:
- calculate_completeness() evaluated at flow creation
- user_type = 'returning' if Master Profile exists with completeness ≥ 0.3
- user_type = 'new' if no profile or completeness < 0.3
- Returning users: cv_import step skipped, flow starts at jd_analysis or gap_analysis
- New users: full flow from jd_analysis → cv_import onwards
- user_type stored on flow_session for downstream logic (e.g., interview MODE A vs MODE B)

User Story Title: Resume an abandoned flow from the dashboard
Description: As a user, I want to resume an abandoned application flow from my dashboard so I don't lose progress.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Dashboard shows active/incomplete flow sessions with current step
- User clicks 'Resume' to re-enter the flow at the last completed step
- Flow state fully restored (current_step, artifacts, interview progress)
- Expired flows (>30 days inactive) are auto-closed by Retention Worker
- Closed flows can be restarted but not resumed

Description: As a user or agent, I need to poll the CV generation status so I know when my tailored CV is ready for download.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GET /api/cv/{cv_id}/status returns {cv_id, status, progress_pct, error_message, download_url}
- Status values: pending, generating, ready, failed
- Frontend polls every 2 seconds with exponential backoff
- MCP tool get_cv_status(cv_id) wraps the same endpoint
- When status = 'ready': download_url populated, expires_at set
- When status = 'failed': error_message populated with user-friendly explanation
- Timeout after 120 seconds triggers automatic failure status

User Story Title: Automated deletion of expired uploads (7-day TTL)
Description: As the system, I need to automatically delete uploaded CV files after 7 days to comply with GDPR data minimisation requirements (ADR-005).
Acceptance Criteria:
- Retention Worker runs as daily cron job via Docker Compose
- Scans uploads table for rows where expires_at < NOW()
- Deletes file from StorageProvider (local filesystem or S3)
- Deletes row from uploads table
- Deletion logged in audit_log with: table, record_id, deletion_reason, timestamp
- Worker is idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Failed deletions retry on next run with alert after 3 failures

User Story Title: Automated deletion of expired interview sessions (30-day TTL)
Description: As the system, I need to automatically delete interview session data after 30 days to comply with GDPR data minimisation requirements (ADR-005).
Acceptance Criteria:
- Retention Worker scans interview_sessions for rows where created_at + 30 days < NOW()
- Deletes session data including: questions, answers, conflict resolutions
- Extracted enrichments already persisted to Master Profile are NOT deleted
- Deletion logged in audit_log
- Associated flow_session marked as closed if still open

User Story Title: Automated deletion of expired CVs (90-day TTL)
Description: As the system, I need to automatically delete generated CVs after their TTL expires (90 days for human, 24 hours for agent) to comply with GDPR (ADR-005).
Acceptance Criteria:
- Retention Worker scans generated_cvs for rows where expires_at < NOW()
- Deletes PDF file from StorageProvider
- Deletes row from generated_cvs table
- User notified on next dashboard visit that CV has expired
- Regeneration option available from flow session
- Deletion logged in audit_log

Description: As the system, I need to soft-delete user accounts after 24 months of inactivity so we comply with GDPR storage limitation (ADR-005).
Acceptance Criteria:
- Retention Worker scans users table for last_active_at + 24 months < NOW()
- Account soft-deleted: is_active = false, anonymized
- Master Profile data anonymized (PII stripped, career data retained as aggregate)
- User notified via email 30 days before soft-delete (reminder email)
- User can reactivate account within 90-day grace period by logging in
- After grace period: hard-delete triggered
- Deletion logged in audit_log

Description: As the system, I need a robust MCP Server layer so that AI agents (e.g., Kaile) can discover, authenticate, and call all user-facing functionality as MCP tools.
Acceptance Criteria:
- MCP Server registers all apliqa.mcp tools corresponding to user-facing API endpoints
- Auth context: API Key resolved to user_id for every tool call
- MCP Transport: stdio for Community edition, SSE for Cloud edition (ADR-010)
- Standardized error responses: invalid calls, auth failures, internal errors
- Tool schemas published for agent discovery
- Every user-facing API endpoint has a corresponding MCP tool exposure
- Rate limiting applied per API key (ADR-007)

User Story Title: MCP tool coverage for all user workflows
Description: As an agent-channel user, I need every user-facing workflow to be accessible via MCP tools so agents can perform the complete application flow on my behalf.
Acceptance Criteria:
- MCP tools implemented for: analyze_jd, analyze_gaps, start_interview, answer_question, generate_cv, get_cv_status, get_profile, update_profile
- Each tool mirrors its REST API counterpart with identical input/output schemas
- Tools enforce same authorization and validation as REST endpoints
- Agent flow validated end-to-end: analyze_jd → analyze_gaps → generate_cv → get_cv_status
- Tools documented with descriptions, parameter schemas, and example calls
- Error responses structured for agent recovery (retry vs abort)

Description: As the system, I need to enforce edition boundaries so Cloud-only features are not accessible in the Community edition (ADR-007/012).
Acceptance Criteria:
- APLIQA_EDITION environment variable checked at startup (community | cloud)
- Cloud-only endpoints return HTTP 402 with upgrade prompt when called in Community
- Cloud-only features: Recruiter Intelligence, SSE transport, S3 storage, advanced analytics
- Namespace separation: Cloud-only code in apliqa.cloud.* package
- Edition check is a decorator/middleware, not scattered through business logic
- Community edition fully functional without Cloud dependencies

Description: As a recruiter, I want the system to intelligently infer regulatory competencies from project descriptions so candidates are positioned accurately for pharma/life science roles.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Cloud-only feature (edition-gated behind APLIQA_EDITION=cloud)
- Infers regulatory knowledge from project context (e.g., MES project work → GAMP 5, batch record systems → 21 CFR Part 11)
- Inference confidence levels: high (≥80%), medium (50–79%), low (<50%)
- Low-confidence inferences flagged for user confirmation during interview
- High-confidence inferences auto-applied to Master Profile with enrichment_record audit
- Domain knowledge base covers: GAMP 5, 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, ICH Q-guidelines, FDA Part 820, ISO 13485, MDR/IVDR, CSV/CSA frameworks
- Key monetisation differentiator for Cloud edition

Description: As a user, I want to start an interview on desktop and finish on mobile (or vice versa) so I can work flexibly
Acceptance Criteria:
- Session state persisted in backend
- User can switch devices mid-interview
- Progress preserved
- No data loss

User Story Title: Generate mock interview questions based on JD + Master Profile
Description: As a user, I want to practice answering interview questions specific to my target role so I can prepare effectively.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System generates 5-10 role-specific interview questions based on JD + Master Profile
- Questions cover: technical skills, behavioral scenarios, industry-specific knowledge, regulatory frameworks (if applicable)
- Questions are open-ended and realistic
- User can answer via text or voice (voice optional, Phase 2.1)
- Estimated time shown upfront (1 min per question)
- Session state persisted; user can resume later

User Story Title: Evaluate candidate responses and provide feedback
Description: As the system, I need to evaluate user responses to mock interview questions and provide constructive feedback.
Acceptance Criteria:
- LLM evaluates response against expected competencies
- Feedback includes: strengths, areas for improvement, suggested talking points
- Confidence score provided (0-100%)
- Feedback stored for future reference
- User can request follow-up questions on weak areas

Description: As a user, I want to see my interview practice history and improvement over time.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Dashboard shows: interviews completed, average confidence score, trending improvement
- Breakdown by topic (technical, behavioral, industry-specific)
- Ability to re-take interviews and compare scores
- Export practice report (PDF)

User Story Title: Display Profile Completeness Score and Interview Readiness Score
Description: As a user, I want to see my Profile Completeness and Interview Readiness scores so I know how prepared I am.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile Completeness Score: 0-100%, updated after each profile edit
- Interview Readiness Score: 0-100%, based on mock interview performance
- Both scores displayed prominently on dashboard
- Breakdown shown: which sections are complete, which need work
- Suggestions for improvement linked to each score

Description: As a user, I want to earn badges for completing key milestones so I feel motivated to use the platform.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Badges awarded for: First CV generated, 5 CVs generated, Profile 100% complete, First mock interview, 10 mock interviews, Interview score >80%
- Badges displayed on profile and dashboard
- Optional: Share badges on LinkedIn
- Leaderboard (optional, Phase 2.2): Top performers by region/industry

Description: As a user, I want to see my application streak so I stay motivated to apply regularly.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Counter shows: consecutive days with at least 1 CV generated
- Displayed on dashboard with visual indicator (fire emoji, etc.)
- Streak resets if user doesn't apply for 2+ days
- Notifications: 'Keep your streak alive!' reminders

User Story Title: Suggest career paths based on Master Profile and market data
Description: As a user, I want to see potential career trajectories based on my experience so I can plan my next move.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System analyzes Master Profile and identifies 2-3 potential career paths
- Each path shows: typical progression, required skills, salary ranges, job market demand
- Paths are informational only (not professional career counseling)
- Disclaimer: 'This is informational guidance, not professional advice'
- User can explore each path and see related job recommendations

User Story Title: Identify skill gaps for target career path
Description: As a user, I want to know what skills I need to develop to move into my target career path.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System compares Master Profile against target career path requirements
- Identifies: missing skills, certifications, experience gaps
- Suggests: training courses, certifications, projects to build experience
- Links to external resources (Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, etc.) - optional affiliate links (transparent)
- Estimated time to close each gap

Description: As a user, I want to see training and certification recommendations to advance my career.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System recommends: certifications, courses, bootcamps relevant to target path
- Recommendations filtered by: industry, career level, time commitment, cost
- Links to training providers (optional affiliate model, transparent)
- Estimated ROI for each recommendation (salary increase potential)
- Disclaimer: 'Affiliate links may be present. We recommend based on relevance, not commission.'

User Story Title: Curated job recommendations based on Master Profile
Description: As a user, I want to see job recommendations that match my profile so I don't have to search manually.
Acceptance Criteria:
- System analyzes Master Profile and recommends 5-10 jobs per week
- Recommendations filtered by: location, salary range, industry, career level, regulatory expertise (if applicable)
- Each recommendation shows: match score, why it's a good fit, salary range, company info
- User can save jobs, dismiss recommendations, adjust filters
- Requires integration with job APIs (LinkedIn, Indeed, etc.) or manual curation

Description: As a user, I want to prepare all application documents (CV, cover letter, etc.) with one click so I can apply faster.
Acceptance Criteria:
- User clicks 'Apply' on a recommended job
- System automatically generates: tailored CV, cover letter, optional cover note
- All documents ready for download or direct submission
- User can review and edit before submitting
- Submission tracked in pipeline

Description: As a user, I want the system to submit applications on my behalf so I can apply to more jobs with less effort.
Acceptance Criteria:
- User grants permission for autonomous submissions
- System submits tailored CV + cover letter directly to job platform
- Submission tracked with timestamp, job details, confirmation
- User notified of submission status
- LEGAL REQUIREMENT: Requires explicit user consent, clear audit trail, ability to revoke
- REGULATORY REQUIREMENT: May require legal review for employment placement regulations (§ 296 SGB III)
